creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
Alternatively, remove the port. It's a simple Python script, easy to run outside of ports. Without dogfooding and/or an upstream liaison there's no point. Worse, it's under GPLv2+ to hinder adoption and code sharing with other projects.

  $ xwayland-run -- foo

is equivalent to

  $ cage Xwayland -noreset :10
  $ DISPLAY=:10 foo

or as one-liner

  $ DISPLAY=:$(Xwayland -terminate -displayfd 1 2>/dev/null &) foo

See also
https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/issues/186
https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-x11/2023-May/003312.html

xwayland-run doesn't even try to prevent various GUI toolkits from bypassing rootful Xwayland, creating windows (directly on Cage or parent compositor) that X11 WM within cannot control. This can be avoided by injecting "env -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY" but then why use xwayland-run if it doesn't help running apps within X11 WMs?
